frameworks.name: conditional policy compression
frameworks.one_line: chunking reduces cognitive cost by conditioning policy on extra information

frameworks.name: rate-distortion / reward-complexity trade-off
frameworks.one_line: chunks emerge from optimizing reward against conditional policy complexity

frameworks.name: working memory capacity limits
frameworks.one_line: scarce cognitive resources drive higher degrees of policy compression

core_question: Why and under what conditions does action chunking occur during sequential skill learning?
key_assumptions: policies have a measurable conditional complexity cost
agents optimize a reward versus policy-complexity trade-off
temporal structure in the environment can be leveraged for action selection
cognitive resources for policy storage are limited
manipulations: presence vs absence of chunkable sub-sequences
key_findings: chunking reduces conditional policy complexity and reaction times
chunking increases with working memory load
chunking reduces overall working memory load, freeing resources for non-chunked information
interpretations: action chunking originates from optimizing the reward versus conditional-policy-complexity trade-off
the degree of policy compression scales with the scarcity of cognitive resources
